title: Concurrency Rises from the Grave
wikitext:
[]: Concurrency Rises from the Grave             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 02, 2011 2:34 am NemZ wrote: Most of it seems to flow naturally out of the situation but it strains at points towards the end, I'll grant you that. You can fanwank around it, but it isn't entirely satisfying. Say, perhaps she wasn't negating Zeruel's ATF as she herself questioned, or maybe it just isn't able to effectively attack at close range? The MPE series regenerating is actually far less effective than things we've seen 01 do... hell, Yui even uses ATF-voodoo to fix the paintjob on her arm while she's at it. By comparison the MPEs do a really halfassed job.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 2:42 am Well, like I said... unsatisfying. What does any of this have to do with hating Shinji though? Seems like it ought to be moved to the overrated Asuka thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:00 am Bagheera wrote: It's not odd, it's just what the word means. If a trait's essential for someone to be called a human then anything that doesn't have the trait isn't human.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:05 am NemZ wrote: Well, like I said... unsatisfying. What does any of this have to do with hating Shinji though? Seems like it ought to be moved to the overrated Asuka thread.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:48 am I'm not getting into the whole concurrency thing yet again when there's absolutely nothing else to say about it that hasn't been said several times before. Go read the old threads if you want to see my thoughts on the matter. That's all just a tangent anyway. The point is that in my view the two are separate, so in order to understand where I'm coming from on Shinji's characterization you have to be able to look at it without allowing cross-pollination of ideas. If you can't do that there's no reason to continue this discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:51 am NemZ wrote: The point is that in my view the two are separate,

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:58 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Yes, but are they actually separate or only separate to you because you want them to be? When I first finished the series I had no preference for them being concurrent, complimentary, or different, but after all the threads I read I determined that the evidence for the "complimentary and occasionally concurrent" side far outweighed that of the others.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 4:10 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Yes, but are they actually separate or only separate to you because you want them to be?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 4:22 am NemZ wrote: The same question could be posed to concurrency theorists.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 4:27 am Then either you didn't read what Tines and I (and anyone else who agreed with us) were saying or it didn't sway you. Regardless, my answer is the same: go read the old threads again or just be content with the difference of opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 4:35 am NemZ wrote: so, adding to what Bagheera said, what exactly would you hope to accomplish? I've already told you my reasons for hating EoE Shinji go beyond the show itself, thus even if you convince me that he and the version of Shinji I like are the same all that will result is giving me reason to retroactively hate the other Shinji too.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 5:03 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: This is step two of my personal Instrumentalizing of you.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 02, 2011 5:15 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I find it amazing that both you and Tines are coming at it from the perspective of "we don't like EoE Shinji, so we don't want to consider him/it to be part of NGE's/EoTV continuity, so we'll consider it a different ending".

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 5:37 am Mr. Tines wrote: The complementation sequences in EoTV are -- especially in Shinji's case -- encouraging, like a talking cure. In EoE, it is all violence (kicking and strangling) and vitriol (all the various break-up lines), against which the few pious platitudes from the demigods hardly stand.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Jan 02, 2011 5:54 am He means that though Ritsuko and Misato are dead, and that they died in the same places as EoE, we don't necessarily know how they got into that state. Not that I agree with him. I just don't wanna argue.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 7:29 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: How much more evidence do you want? Like I told NemZ, I covered this more comprehensively in another post

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 02, 2011 9:08 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: How much more evidence do you want?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 10:26 am NemZ wrote: I don't see any evidence, just epic-scale fanwanking. You're cutting apart two self-contained works and stitching them together into a frankenstein's monster full of redundancies and discrepancies.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 10:53 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: You have not pointed out one "discrepancy" in my analysis thus far.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Jan 02, 2011 11:35 am Following up what tines said about complementary endings, a way to simplify that is to say that the TV ending was one where you feel good for Shinji, and EoE is the ending where you pity him and hope he turns out well. It could be a continuation of the kabbalic duality themes featured all through evangelion.

You can fanwank around it, but it isn't entirely satisfying. Say, perhaps she wasn't negating Zeruel's ATF as she herself questioned, or maybe it just isn't able to effectively attack at close r
